text: Follow-up on ms-shift-spy-v1 walk-forward (overall Sharpe 0.674, failed threshold; per-fold Sharpes 0.313, 0.428, 1.45, -0.32, 1.45): the negative fold plausibly lines up with the 2022 bear market (see brain/raw/ms-shift-spy-v1-fold-regime-hypothesis.md for the calendar-based approximation and reasoning), but the current 5-fold split is too coarse to confirm this from the existing result alone. Re-run the walk-forward backtest for ms-shift-spy-v1 (same manifest, same 2016-01-01..2024-12-31 period, same snapshot) with a finer fold count (folds=10 or folds=12 in run_backtest) so each fold covers roughly 4-5 months instead of ~22 months. If the weakness is genuinely concentrated in the 2022 rate-hike/bear-market window, the finer-grained fold Sharpes should show a contiguous cluster of poor/negative folds landing on calendar dates inside 2022, with the folds on either side (2020-21 recovery, 2023-24 rally) staying strong. If instead the weak folds are scattered across the finer split with no regime-aligned clustering, that would falsify the regime-dependence hypothesis and point back toward a uniformly weak/absent edge. This is a pure re-run with an existing param (no code change needed) and directly tests whether the strategy's edge is regime-conditional before investing effort in a regime filter or param changes.
